posts.text:
BREAKING: A record 6.0% of workers in Vanguard-managed 401(k) plans took hardship withdrawals in 2025.

This is up from 4.6% in 2024 and has nearly QUADRUPLED since 2020.

Hardship withdrawals have increased for 6 consecutive years, with last year's median withdrawal being ~$1,900.

The most common reasons were avoiding foreclosure or eviction and covering medical costs.

Financial pressure on US households is surging at an alarming pace.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
